name: Arlo Pro 6 Wireless Security Camera
verdict: The best overall home security camera — 2K HDR, color night vision, and the most intelligent AI detection available.
description: The Arlo Pro 6 is CNET's top pick for 2026, and it earns that title with the most intelligent detection system in any home camera. It distinguishes between people, animals, vehicles, and packages — sending specific alerts so you're not bombarded by every passing car. 2K HDR video captures sharp detail day and night, and the built-in spotlight enables full-color night vision. The magnetic mount goes anywhere in seconds, and the rechargeable battery lasts months. Works with Alexa, Google, and Apple HomeKit. Local storage via USB is free; cloud plans are optional.
features: 2K HDR video with color night vision
AI detection: people, animals, vehicles, packages
Works with Alexa, Google, and Apple HomeKit
Local storage option — no subscription required
cons: $150 per camera adds up for multi-cam setups
Best features require Arlo Secure subscription

name: Wyze Cam v4 Indoor/Outdoor Security Camera
verdict: The best budget security camera — 2.5K resolution, color night vision, and local storage for just $36.
description: The Wyze Cam v4 is proof that good security doesn't have to be expensive. At $36, it delivers 2.5K resolution, color night vision with a built-in spotlight, IP65 weather resistance for indoor or outdoor use, and free local storage via microSD card. Motion detection zones are customizable, and two-way audio lets you communicate through the camera. The Wyze app provides live viewing, playback, and smart alerts. No subscription is required for basic features. With 10K+ bought monthly on Amazon, it's the most popular budget security camera for a reason.
pros: Best security camera value on the market
2.5K resolution at this price is remarkable
No subscription needed for core features
cons: Wired power only — no battery option
Advanced AI features require Cam Plus subscription
App can be slow to load live view

name: Ring Outdoor Cam Plus Battery
verdict: The best Ring camera — 2K video, color night vision, and seamless Alexa integration with Ring's trusted ecosystem.
description: Ring is the most recognized name in home security, and the Outdoor Cam Plus is their best all-around camera. 2K video provides clear detail for identifying faces and license plates. The battery-powered design means no wiring — mount it anywhere with the included tools. Ring's app is the most polished security camera app available, with easy live viewing, event timeline, and sharing. Alexa integration lets you view cameras on Echo Show devices with a voice command. Ring Protect subscription ($4/month) unlocks video history and person detection.
features: Ring app — best security camera app
cons: Best features require Ring Protect subscription
No Google Home or HomeKit support

name: Google Nest Cam (Battery) Indoor/Outdoor
verdict: The best camera for Google Home — intelligent alerts, 3 hours free cloud storage, and seamless Google ecosystem integration.
description: The Google Nest Cam Battery is the camera for Google Home households. Intelligent alerts distinguish between people, animals, and vehicles without a subscription. Three hours of free cloud event storage means you get basic recording without paying monthly. The magnetic mount attaches anywhere, and the weatherproof design works indoors or out. The Google Home app provides a unified view of all your Nest devices. The camera is also the only option that works with Google's Family Bell and household routines. HDR video quality is good but not the sharpest at 1080p.
cons: Nest Aware subscription needed for full history

name: Blink Outdoor 4 Wireless Security Camera
verdict: The best battery life — 2 years on AA batteries with no wiring, no charging, and Alexa integration.
description: The Blink Outdoor 4 solves the biggest annoyance with battery cameras: recharging. It runs on two AA lithium batteries for up to 2 years. Set it up, forget about it, and it just works. 1080p video is adequate for monitoring (not the sharpest for detail), and infrared night vision covers after dark. The Sync Module stores clips locally on a USB drive — no subscription needed. At $80 (often sold in multi-packs for less per camera), it's the most affordable way to cover multiple zones around your home. Amazon-owned, so Alexa integration is seamless.
features: Local storage via Sync Module USB
pros: Local storage means no subscription needed
cons: Sync Module required for local storage

name: eufy SoloCam S350 Outdoor Security Camera
verdict: The best no-subscription camera — 4K dual-lens with 8x zoom, solar panel, and 100% local storage.
description: eufy has built its brand on one promise: no subscriptions, ever. The SoloCam S350 delivers 4K resolution from dual lenses — a wide-angle lens for monitoring and a telephoto lens with 8x hybrid zoom for detail. The built-in solar panel keeps the battery topped up indefinitely in most climates. All footage stores locally on the 8GB built-in eMMC storage. AI person detection, auto-tracking, and two-way audio work without any monthly fee. For users who refuse to pay subscriptions, eufy is the only premium option.
pros: Zero subscription costs — everything is free
4K dual-lens zoom is unique at this price

name: Ring Indoor Cam Plus (2nd Gen)
verdict: The best indoor camera — compact, 2K video, privacy shutter, and Ring's trusted app for $50.
description: The Ring Indoor Cam Plus is the simplest way to monitor inside your home. At $50, it delivers 2K video, a physical privacy shutter (flip it closed when you're home), two-way audio, and motion detection. The compact size fits on any shelf or mounts on a wall. Ring's app provides live viewing, event history, and the ability to share clips. Pre-roll video capture shows what happened before motion triggered — crucial for understanding events. It's wired (USB-C), so no battery concerns. The most popular indoor camera on Amazon with 40,000+ reviews.
features: Ring app with live view and sharing
cons: Ring Protect needed for video history

name: Reolink Argus 4 Pro Solar Security Camera
verdict: The best 4K solar camera — 180-degree dual-lens view, solar powered, and no subscription with local + cloud storage.
description: The Reolink Argus 4 Pro combines a 180-degree ultra-wide view with 4K resolution from dual lenses — it covers the area of two regular cameras in one. The included solar panel keeps it perpetually charged, and the 8GB onboard storage plus free Reolink Cloud (1GB) means no subscription is needed. Color night vision with spotlights, two-way audio, and smart person/vehicle detection complete the package. The Reolink app is straightforward and reliable. For users who want maximum coverage with minimum cameras and zero ongoing costs, this is the pick.
pros: Solar + local storage = zero ongoing costs
cons: Dual-lens image can look distorted at edges
App is functional but not polished

name: TP-Link Tapo C420 Outdoor Security Camera
verdict: The best value outdoor camera — 2K QHD, starlight sensor, and local storage for $100 with no subscription.
description: TP-Link's Tapo line has quickly become the go-to for budget security cameras, and the C420 is their best outdoor model. 2K QHD resolution with a starlight sensor captures usable footage even in near-darkness without spotlights. The rechargeable battery lasts 180 days, and the magnetic mount installs in seconds. All footage stores locally on a microSD card — no subscription needed. The Tapo app is clean and responsive with live view, playback, activity zones, and smart detection. At $100 with zero ongoing costs, it undercuts Ring and Blink while matching their core features.
cons: Smart detection is basic vs Arlo

name: Ring Floodlight Cam Wired Plus
verdict: The best floodlight camera — 2000 lumens, 1080p video, and motion-activated security lighting in one device.
description: The Ring Floodlight Cam replaces your existing outdoor floodlight with a security camera and smart lighting system. 2000 lumens of LED floodlights activate on motion, deterring intruders while the 1080p camera records everything. Two-way audio with a built-in siren adds another layer of security. The floodlights are adjustable for coverage angle, and motion zones let you customize sensitivity. Hardwired installation means no battery concerns. It's the most popular floodlight camera on Amazon with 42,000+ reviews. Replaces two devices (floodlight + camera) with one.
pros: Replaces floodlight + camera with one device
cons: Ring Protect needed for video history
